heading: Creation; composition; appointment and tenure; meetings
label: 2501 Creation; composition; appointment and tenure; meetings
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., §§ 391 (part), 393(a) (, title I, § 104, , and title V, § 503(a) as added , ).June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 381Sept. 5, 1950, ch. 849, § 6(d)64 Stat. 583This section incorporates only the last sentence of paragraph (b) of former section 391. The balance of that section will be found in sections 1506, 2102, 2301, and 2902 of the revision.

Amendments2008—Subsec. (b)(1). , inserted “not more than 2” after “subsection (a) shall be appointed for” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 110–404, § 3(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(1)(A). , substituted “not more than 4 terms” for “a term”.Pub. L. 110–404, § 3(a)(1)(B)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 110–404, § 3(b)(1)1988— substituted “appointment and tenure; meetings” for “appointment and tenure” in section catchline, and amended text generally, revising and restating as subsecs. (a) to (c) provisions formerly contained in a single undesignated paragraph.Pub. L. 100–3651984— struck out “The authority of the Administrator of General Services under to regroup, transfer, and distribute functions within the General Services Administration does not extend to the Commission or its functions.”Pub. L. 98–497section 754 of title 401979— inserted “and Records” after “Publications”.Pub. L. 96–981974— inserted provision relating to appointment and term of two members of Society of American Archivists, and two members of American Association for State and Local History.Pub. L. 93–5361972— provided for two additional members of the Organization of American Historians to be appointed for terms of four years by Executive Board of Organization, one to be appointed for a term of two years and his successors for a term of four years.Pub. L. 92–546

Effective Date of 2008 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–404, § 3(a)(2)122 Stat. 4282Oct. 13, 2008“The restrictions on the terms of members of the National Historical Publications and Records Commission provided in the amendments made by paragraph (1) [amending this section] shall apply to members serving on or after the date of enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 13, 2008, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–404, § 3(b)(2)122 Stat. 4283Oct. 13, 2008“The requirement of recusal provided in the amendment made by paragraph (1) [amending this section] shall apply to members of the National Historical Publications and Records Commission serving on or after the date of enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 13, 2008

Effective Date of 1988 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–365, § 2(b)102 Stat. 824July 13, 1988“The amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall be effective on , and shall apply to the appointment of any member on the expiration of a predecessor’s term as follows:January 1, 1989“(1)The next two members appointed to such Commission after such date shall be appointed pursuant to section 2501(a)(2)(E) and (F) of title 44, United States Code, as amended by this section.“(2)Notwithstanding section 2501(b)(1), the first members appointed pursuant to section 2501(a)(2)(B) and (C) after , shall be appointed for terms of one year.”January 1, 1991

children.label: A
children.text: the Archivist of the United States, who shall be chairman;

children.label: B
children.text: the Librarian of Congress (or an alternate designated by the Librarian);

children.label: C
children.text: one Senator, appointed by the President of the Senate;

children.label: D
children.text: one Representative, appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives;

children.label: E
children.text: one member of the judicial branch of the Government, appointed by the Chief Justice of the United States;

children.label: F
children.text: one representative of the Department of State to be appointed by the Secretary of State; and

children.label: G
children.text: one representative of the Department of Defense to be appointed by the Secretary of Defense;

children.label: D
children.text: the American Association for State and Local History;

children.label: E
children.text: the Association for Documentary Editing; and

children.label: F
children.text: the National Association for Government Archives and Records Administrators; and
two other members, outstanding in the fields of the social or physical sciences, the arts, or archival or library science, appointed by the President of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: a member appointed under subsection (a)(1)(D) shall be appointed for not more than 4 terms of 2 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Archivist and the Librarian of Congress are permanent ex officio members.
A member may continue to serve after the expiration of a term until a successor has been appointed, but not to exceed one year.

children.label: c
children.text: The Commission shall meet at least annually and at call of the Chairman.

children.heading: .—Recusal
children.label: d .—Recusal
children.text: Members of the Commission shall recuse themselves from voting on any matter that poses, or could potentially pose, a conflict of interest, including a matter that could benefit them or an entity they represent.
